summary: The checkout has no commits ahead of origin/main and no recent merge references #3265. Existing code preserves images during job cleanup and records backend-scoped image observations, but the deployment-level maintenance operation itself is absent: there are no maintenance settings, inventory, watermark planner, protected-candidate eviction executor, separate schedule, reports, or pressure diagnostics. New code work is required.

requirements.description: Image maintenance is a separate deployment operation from job cleanup and is dry-run/disabled by conservative default.
Observe disk pressure and plan eviction only above a configured high-water threshold.
Stop eviction at the low-water target, pass limit, or absence of safe candidates.
Order candidates using backend-scoped last-used observations and minimum retention age.
Protect active/starting/service, pinned/protected, young, and ambiguous/unowned images.
Re-inspect candidates immediately before deletion and safely skip races.
Handle tag/digest aliases without accidentally deleting protected images.
Emit reviewable dry-run and execution reports covering candidates, skips, deletions, failures, bytes, and remaining pressure.
Job completion and cleanup do not invoke image maintenance or delete shared images.
No global image-prune command is used by container-job cleanup.
Expose operator diagnostics/readiness/metrics for pressure and inability to find safe candidates.
Isolate inventory and last-used state by configured backend reference.
